entry.title: Re: Customer Invoice workflow in the public cloud Finance
entry.content: Hi Varun, I've reviewed the preliminary billing documents, which fall under the SD module. However, since we don't have SD in scope, I'm looking to set up a workflow specifically for customer invoice approval within the Finance module.

entry.title: Re: Integration of SAP TM with SAP GTS: Outbound movement
entry.content: Thanks for this article. Could you please share what all config steps are required in TM side and GTS side to achieve this functionality.

entry.title: Re: Receiving Electronic Invoices by Email in SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, or SAP
entry.content:
Hello Ms. Wolf,

you mention the automatic extraction of the xml from the email by the business system. Could you elaborate how the business system receives and accesses the email to ultimately extract the xml. You description and the SAP documentation imply that the SAP system acts as a email inbox. However I am rather sceptical about that. 

Do you maybe have more information to illustrate the subject? 

Thank you and kind regards, 

Konstantin

entry.title: For ECC clients what will happen to the Graphical Planning Table after December 2025?
entry.content:
We have a client on ECC and so far they have no plan to migrate to S/4HANA.  They want to implement the Graphical Planning Table (transaction CM21/CM25).  What risks will they face in doing so?  Does the S/4HANA's Compatibility Scope deadline of 31 December 2025 impact ECC systems too?  If they discover a bug in CM21/CM25 will SAP support them during 2026 and 2027?  

Thank you!  Ann Hustis

MAN Production Planning (PP)

entry.title: Re: Commit work in exit Workflow
entry.content: By definition (as per ABAP documentation), a GTT must be cleared before database commit so I don't understand how you can see data in the table...

entry.title: Re: SAP Build and ABAP Cloud Basic Trial Offering
entry.content:
Hey @Andre_Fischer 
Thanks for this great workbook. 

A small fix: At the "Add the creation of sales orders in SAP S/4 HANA Cloud" part of tutorial, there is a subtitle "Exercise 4.5: Add an additional save". I have to inherit from cl_abap_behavior_saver_failed, to make things work. If I use the cl_abap_behavior_saver class, i couldn't make the redefinition of "SAVE_MODIFIED" method in the local class. I've already checked the base class, btw.

In the cl_abap_behavior_saver class there is no "failed" parameter, and perplexity AI says that's the reason.

Cheers

entry.title: Re: SAP BDC for Dummies
entry.content: Good Summarization Swati

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Advanced Returns Management (ARM): A Basic Guide
entry.content: @AK_75  You can configure a new follow-up activity where the returned goods are directly moved to the scrap storage location, or you can design a process with movement type 551. The stock can then be consumed or further processed by MM consultants depending on the business needs.

entry.title: Re: SAP Commerce Cloud, Composable Storefront Support wit...
entry.content:
Dear Adam

YES. SAP Commerce Cloud Composable Storefront will maintain compatibility with the August 2025 Core Commerce release for the same 12-month period and not revert to the typical six-month release cycle in this cas.

SAP has extended the August 2025 release of SAP Commerce Cloud Core Commerce to 12 months of mainstream maintenance, instead of the usual six-month update cycle.

SAP Composable Storefront, formerly known as Spartacus, has been following an aligned versioning and support strategy with Core Commerce since early 2024. This alignment includes synchronizing release timing, compatibility, and support duration. Composable Storefront receives monthly updates for features and bug fixes, and a yearly Angular framework upgrade typically takes place in February. In addition, bugfix-only periods are observed during December and January.

Because the August 2025 Core Commerce release benefits from a 12-month support window, the Composable Storefront release aligned to it will also maintain compatibility for the full 12-month period. This ensures customers can rely on long-term compatibility and coordinated maintenance across both the Core Commerce backend and the Composable frontend.

Therefore, the answer is yes — SAP Commerce Cloud Composable Storefront will maintain compatibility with the August 2025 Core Commerce release for the same 12-month period and not revert to the typical six-month release cycle in this cas

  • Best regards

entry.title: Re: How to get the link to an Odata service when creating...
entry.content:
When you are an ABAP developer, you could do this tutorial:

https://developers.sap.com/mission.sap-fiori-abap-rap100.html

In this tutorial you create an OData service yourself.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build and ABAP Cloud Basic Trial Offering
entry.content:
It seems that you have misunderstood something.

Once you have changed the code in the behavior definition such

managed with additional save implementation in class ZBP_R_AC000000U## unique;

You will be able to add the implementation of an empty saver class using Ctrl+1.

The empty saver class will look as follows, and if you look closely at the code snippets for the implementation of the method save_modified(), you won't see any usage of "failed". 

Kind regards,

Andre

 

CLASS lsc_zr_ac000000u## DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_saver.

  PROTECTED SECTION.

    METHODS save_modified REDEFINITION.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS lsc_zr_ac000000u## IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD save_modified.
 
  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

entry.title: Re: How to get the link to an Odata service when creating...
entry.content:
@Bucule 

you can find one of my repo with Odata sample

https://github.com/yogananda-muthaiah/Fiori_Odata_sample

entry.title: Re: Session on Input Service Distribution (ISD) functionality in SAP
entry.content:
@Sruja transactions in S/4HANA do not need to be uploaded manually.

The same shall be auto-picked in GSTR-6 generated in DCS. 

 

Regards

Faizan

entry.title: Re: EWM - Vendor Returns Process - Returns Delivery Vs Returns PO
entry.content:
Hi Van, thank you very much in advance for sharing so much valuable information.
I have a question: When I try to activate a BC set, I'm getting a dump. How can I find out which BC sets need to be activated first?

Thank you very much. I'll be waiting.

Wilson V.

entry.title: Actual Activity Rate Calculation without using Material Ledger Actual Costing (33Q)
entry.content:
Question:
In a "Make to stock" production accounting scenario, where the Scope Item 33Q(Actual Costing) is not activated, how do we calculate the "Actual Activity rates" and post "Activity Price variances" on Price Difference Account?

(SAP S4HANA Public Cloud)

Detail:

If actual Costing (33Q) is NOT activated, and the scenario is as follows:

  1. It’s a Make to Stock Scenario  where the Finished Good and the components (RM and SFG) are all valuated using the Price Control "S".
  • When the goods are issued to the Production Order, the posting is done based on the Standard Price of the Materials. To be precise: "Actual Material Quantity * Standard Price".
  • When the Activity Confirmation happens on the Production Order, the posting is done based on the valuation variant define in the Production Order type. In our case it is the Planned Period Rates for Activities. Thus, "Actual Activity Quantity * Planned Activity Rate".

 

As we are not running Actual Costing (CKMLCP equivalent) because 33Q is out of scope, I completely understand that the material price variances due to the RM/SFG material price differences are not going to be settled on to the Finished good, as we do not have the Actual Costing activated. Thus, material price variances will remain posted on the price difference account (PRD) for the Raw/SFG Materials.

 

But I would like to understand, how the Activity Price variance (due to the difference between Planned & Actual Activity Rates) are going to be calculated, and captured on the price difference accounts?

entry.title: Re: Commit work in exit Workflow
entry.content: Hi Sandra, the table configuration I was using was indeed incorrect. I was supposed to be working on a database table, not a temporary one. I didn't need to use commit work on my exit, and it worked perfectly! Thank you very much.

entry.title: Re: Quarterly Reporting in S4/HANA Group Reporting Public...
entry.content:
Dear Geeta

Have you tried this?

 

  1. Use of FS Items and Group Reporting Data Collection: Ensure that all financial data is correctly posted and consolidated using ACDOCU.

  2. Reporting Tool: Use SAP Group Reporting Data Collection (GRDC) or SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) as a front-end for reporting, depending on your landscape. Both tools support parameter prompts and dynamic filtering.

  3. Time Selection with Variable Prompts:

    • In SAC, create input prompts or variables for Period, Quarter, and Year. You can implement a logic with restricted measures or calculated measures that adapt based on the selection (e.g. if QTD is selected, sum values from the beginning of the quarter to the current month).
    • In Group Reporting Fiori apps, like "Group Data Analysis", you can create a variant or custom report where period values are dynamically filtered based on the prompt selection using filters and parameters (FISCVAR, FISCYEAR, PERIOD).

  4. Restricted Key Figures: Define restricted key figures for Monthly, QTD, and YTD in the report. You can create:

    • One for Monthly = selected period only
    • One for QTD = sum of periods 1 to selected period within the quarter
    • One for YTD = sum from period 1 to selected period in the year

  5. Custom Logic (if needed): If more complex logic is required, develop a custom CDS View with logic for QTD, using CASE statements and PERIOD filters to compute cumulative figures depending on the quarter.

  6. Optional: If using BW/4HANA or embedded analytics, time-dependent hierarchies and custom structures can be used to provide a similar time navigation experience.

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Eclipse ABAP ADT - New Cloud Project - No option for...
entry.content:
Since November 2024, there is a uniform ABAP Cloud Project popup for SAP BTP ABAP Environment and SAP S/4HANA Cloud. You only need to specify the URL end-point of the respective system.

Please also refer to the tutorial https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-environment-console-application.html

entry.title: Re: Not able to Update Standard dictionary text in SAP En...
entry.content:
Hi Nagaraj,

Before double clicking the dictionary object to open and modify it, right click it and select Server Actions > Start Editing.  You must have editing control of the object, like any Enable Now object, before making the change.  Once you done that and saved the change, don't forget to Finish Editing the object and publish it.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: How to get material number from KSSK table
entry.content: Thanks Raymond - I'll have to get an ABAPer to help me with this but will give it a try .

entry.title: Debugging a Python SAP BTP Service Locally in VSCode
entry.content:
There is a lot of information available about creating and debugging Node.js services in SAP BTP. However, in some cases, we might need to use Python instead — for example, for tasks involving AI or data processing.

In this article, I’ll show you how to debug a Python-based SAP BTP service using VS Code locally.

Prerequisites

Create an SAP BTP Trial Account. Please follow this tutorial for detailed information. 

Add HANA to your SAP BTP trial account if you haven’t already.

To do this, refer to the section ‘Create an SAP HANA Cloud Instance’ of this tutorial.

Note: One of the steps in the tutorial above requires the Space ID.
If you're not sure where to find it, navigate to your space in the SAP BTP Cockpit and copy the Space ID from the URL.

Install Visual Studio Code (VS Code). You can download it from this page.

Install the SAP Cloud Foundry CLI. Refer to this guide on installing the cf CLI for detailed instructions.

Install the Python interpreter. Version 3.13 or higher is recommended.

Install the necessary extensions for VS Code:

  • Python extension by Microsoft
  • Python debugger extension by Microsoft

Note: When installing the first extension (Python), the second one (Python Debugger) may be installed automatically. If not, you can install it manually.

Steps to create a service

Our service will not include authorization for the sake of simplicity, but it will use an SAP HANA service to demonstrate how to call a BTP service from the local environment. We will use the initial setup from this tutorial.

In the following sections, I will replicate some of the steps from that tutorial in this blog post, with a few minor adjustments.

  • In your local file system, create a new folder –  for example, python-debug.
  • In Visual Studio Code, open the python-debug folder.
  • Create a file named manifest.yml with the following content:
---
applications:
  - name: python-debug
    random-route: true
    path: ./
    memory: 128M
    buildpacks:
      - python_buildpack
    command: python server.py
    services:
      - python-debug-hana
​

IMPORTANT: Make sure you don’t already have another application named python-debug in your space. If you do, use a different name and update the entire tutorial accordingly.

  • Specify the Python runtime version your application will use. To do this, create a file named runtime.txt with the following content:
python-3.13.x​
  • This application will be a web server utilizing the Flask web framework. To specify Flask as a dependency, create a file named requirements.txt with the following content:
Flask==2.3.*
cfenv==0.5.3
hdbcli==2.17.*
  • Create a file named server.py with the following application logic:
import os
from flask import Flask
from cfenv import AppEnv
from hdbcli import dbapi

app = Flask(__name__)
env = AppEnv()

hana_service = 'hana'
hana = env.get_service(label=hana_service)

port = int(os.environ.get('PORT', 3000))
@app.route('/')
def hello():
    if hana is None:
        return "Can't connect to HANA service '{}' – check service name?".format(hana_service)
    else:
        conn = dbapi.connect(address=hana.credentials['host'],
                             port=int(hana.credentials['port']),
                             user=hana.credentials['user'],
                             password=hana.credentials['password'],
                             encrypt='true',
                             sslTrustStore=hana.credentials['certificate'])

        cursor = conn.cursor()
        cursor.execute("select CURRENT_UTCTIMESTAMP from DUMMY")
        ro = cursor.fetchone()
        cursor.close()
        conn.close()

        return "Current time is: " + str(ro["CURRENT_UTCTIMESTAMP"])

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run(host='0.0.0.0', port=port)
​

This is a simple server that will return the result of a basic HANA SQL query when requested (in our case, the current UTC time).

  • Log in to Cloud Foundry using the following command in the command line:
cf login​

When prompted, enter your API endpoint. You can find it on the Account Overview page in your SAP BTP account.

Also, enter the registration email and password for your SAP BTP trial account.

  • Create a HANA service using the following command:
cf create-service hana hdi-shared python-debug-hana​
  • Deploy the application on Cloud Foundry. To do that, run the following command inside the python-debug directory:
cf push​

Note: If you make any changes to the service file and want to deploy again, simply run the ‘cf push’ command again.

  • After successful deployment, the python-debug application should start automatically, and its details will be displayed in the command console.
  • Open a browser and enter the application’s generated URL (you can find it under “routes”)
    For example: python-debug-zany-buffalo-zz.cfapps.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com

Steps to debug the service locally

Local debugging of applications running on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) is not only possible, but also practical in many cases. Here’s why:

  • Globally Accessible Services:
    In SAP BTP, all service endpoints use globally routable addresses. This means that your application can access these services from any location, not just from within the SAP BTP environment. As a result, you can run and test your application locally while still interacting with cloud services such as databases, messaging queues, or authentication providers.
  • Minimal Environmental Differences:
    SAP BTP does not provide a highly customized runtime environment. Apart from environment variables—which you can replicate locally—there are no special runtime conditions. This allows developers to simulate the production environment with high fidelity on their local machines by simply setting the appropriate environment variables.

Thanks to these two factors, local development and debugging in SAP BTP is straightforward. It enables faster development cycles and easier troubleshooting without the need to redeploy for every change.

To enable local debugging of a Python-based service, follow these steps:

  • Since the SAP BTP environment provides necessary configuration through environment variables, you need to replicate them locally. In order to load local environment data, insert the following changed code to your Python server script:
import os
from flask import Flask
from cfenv import AppEnv
from hdbcli import dbapi
from dotenv import load_dotenv
load_dotenv()

app = Flask(__name__)
env = AppEnv()

hana_service = 'hana'
hana = env.get_service(label=hana_service)

port = int(os.environ.get('PORT', 3000))
@app.route('/')
def hello():
    if hana is None:
        return "Can't connect to HANA service '{}' – check service name?".format(hana_service)
    else:
        conn = dbapi.connect(address=hana.credentials['host'],
                             port=int(hana.credentials['port']),
                             user=hana.credentials['user'],
                             password=hana.credentials['password'],
                             encrypt='true',
                             sslTrustStore=hana.credentials['certificate'])

        cursor = conn.cursor()
        cursor.execute("select CURRENT_UTCTIMESTAMP from DUMMY")
        ro = cursor.fetchone()
        cursor.close()
        conn.close()

        return "Current time is: " + str(ro["CURRENT_UTCTIMESTAMP"])

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run(host='0.0.0.0', port=port)
​

This new logic will let you automatically read the key-value pairs from your .env file and make them available via os.environ, just like in the cloud environment.

  • Update the requirements.txt to include the necessary libraries
Flask==2.3.*
cfenv==0.5.3
hdbcli==2.17.*
python-dotenv​
  • Create a Python Virtual Environment

    Every Python project should run in its own isolated environment to avoid dependency conflicts.

    In VS Code, open the Command Palette (Ctrl+Shift+P), and choose ‘Python: Create Environment…’
  • Then select the option ‘Venv Creates a ‘.venv’ virtual environment in the current workspace’:
  • Then select a Python Version (Python 3.13.x or higher is recommended):
  • On the next screen, confirm the option to install the dependencies:

Note: You can also do this later using the command pip install -r requirements.txt.

  • A new virtual environment will be created in a .venv folder in your workspace:
  • Set the VCAP_SERVICES environment variable
    Before running the service locally, we need to set the VCAP_SERVICES environment variable, which provides all the necessary information about SAP BTP services.
    To retrieve its contents, use the following command:
cf env python-debug​

Now, copy the contents of the VCAP_SERVICES JSON block to your clipboard.

  • Before we can use the contents of the environment variable, we need to minimize the JSON into a single line. You can use any online JSON minifier tool or the JSTool extension in Notepad++ to do this.
  • Now, create a .env file in your service folder (the same folder where the manifest.yml file is located), and add the environment variable contents to it in the following format:
VCAP_SERVICES=<minimized_json>​

  • Create a configuration in VSCode. Before starting, open the server.py file in the editor.
  • Open the ‘Run and Debug’ section in VSCode and select ‘Create a launch.json file’:

    Note: If you have already created the configuration file, simply open it for edit.

  • From the drop-down list, select ‘Python debugger’
  • Select ‘Python File: Debug the currently active Python file’:
  • In the created file, delete the commented-out lines starting with // to avoid syntax errors
  • Return to the server.py file, and start the debugging session
  • Start the debug session
  • The debugging session is now running:
  • Open the following address in a browser
http://127.0.0.1/​

The current date and time from the HANA server will be displayed

  • Then, return to VSCode and set a break-point
  • Refresh the browser again, and the execution will pause at the breakpoint:

Now, you can use all the Python debugger’s features to debug your program.

entry.title: Re: One Stop Shop of my Knowledge Artifacts in SAP HCM
entry.content:
Hello,

Can I receive access to all files please?

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Actual Activity Rate Calculation without using Materi...
entry.content:
Hello @KaranChauhan 

Thank you for your detailed question regarding the calculation of Actual Activity Rates and treatment of Activity Price Variances in your Make-to-Stock production scenario, specifically where Scope Item 33Q (Actual Costing) is not activated.

I’d like to confirm the following, based on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition standard functionality

System Behaviour without 33Q (Actual Costing)

  • Your materials, including Finished Goods and Components (RM/SFG), are valued with Price Control’s (Standard Price).
  • During production:
    • Goods Issue is posted as Actual Quantity × Standard Price.
    • Activity Confirmation posts as Actual Activity Quantity × Planned Activity Rate.

As you rightly mentioned, without 33Q, there is no Material Ledger Actual Costing (CKMLCP equivalent), and inventory remains valued at standard price throughout.

Treatment of Activity Price Variances

  • You continue to plan Activity Rates via your Cost Center/Activity Type Planning.
  • At period-end, SAP compares actual costs incurred on cost centers against the actual activity quantities confirmed during production.
  • The system calculates an actual activity rate based on this comparison.

The difference between Planned vs. Actual Activity Rates results in an Activity Price Variance, which Is posted to the Price Difference Account (PRD)

- Does not adjust inventory valuation, as Actual Costing (33Q) is not in scope
- Remains visible within financial reporting as a variance impact on P&L

Note

  • Without 33Q
    • Both Material Price Variances and Activity Price Variances are posted to variance (PRD) accounts.
    • There is no inventory revaluation based on actual costs.
    • This approach follows SAP's standard for Public Cloud when Actual Costing is excluded from the project scope.

SAP Help Portal – Product Costing and Overhead Management

SAP KBA 3086829 – Actual Activity Rate Behaviour in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

KBA 3086829 – Actual Activity Rate & Variances in Public Cloud

Key Takeaways

 In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, if Actual Costing (33Q) is not activated:

  • Materials remain valued at Standard Price (S).
  • Activity confirmations use the Planned Activity Rate set in Cost Center/Activity Type Planning.

 At Period-End, the system

  • Compares Planned Activity Rates used during production with actual costs incurred on Cost Centers.
  • Calculates an Actual Activity Rate based on real costs and confirmed activity quantities.

 Variance Handling

  • Differences between Planned and Actual Activity Rates result in Activity Price Variances.
  • These variances are posted to:
    • The Price Difference Account (PRD) for materials.
    • Or remain within the Cost Center/Profitability structure for non-material linked activities.

Without 33Q

  • There is no inventory revaluation, regardless of variances.
  • All variances stay in P&L accounts.

Practical Example

  • You plan Activity Rate = €70/hour
  • Actual costs on Cost Center result in Actual Activity Rate = €75/hour
  • Variance (€5/hour) posted to PRD at period-end — inventory stays valued at standard price

Conclusion

  • This is standard Public Cloud behavior, designed for scenarios where full Material Ledger Actual Costing is not in scope.
  • Variances are visible in financial reports but do not impact stock valuation.

Let me know if you need further support

GenAI was used to help generate this content

entry.title: Re: Blog It Forward - Vivek Barnwal
entry.content:
Hi Vivek,

You shared a lot of info about time management, but now most of them are not available. 

After I read the comments, I realized that the material is verry good for someone new to SAP HCM like me.

Could you please give me access on it?

Regards, 

Catalin

entry.title: Re: Actual Activity Rate Calculation without using Materi...
entry.content:
@Chuma:

      1. You mentioned that the system will calculate the actual activity prices at the month end.
        And then the system will compare the actual prices with the planned prices.
        Can you please explain how the system will do these 2 steps without 33Q being activated? 
      2. Additionally, the SAP note 3086829 which you provided does not pertain to this topic. Can you please check again.

entry.title: Re: How to get material number from KSSK table
entry.content:
Tried this using SQVI and worked . The missing table for me was INOB .This is what I did : 

Step 1: Get CLINT from KLAH

  • Go to table KLAH
  • Enter your batch class name in CLASS
  • Ensure KLART = 023 (023 = Batch class)
  • Note down the CLINT value

 

Step 2: Go to table KSSK

  • Enter:
    • CLINT = value from Step 1
    • KLART = 023
  • This gives you a list of internal object numbers (KSSK-OBJEK) linked to that class

Step 3: Go to table INOB

  • Enter CUOBJ = KSSK-OBJEK
  • Filter:
    • KLART = 023
    • OBTAB = MARA
  • INOB-OBJEK will give you the material numbers

entry.title: Re: Discover the Fresh New Design of SAP Help Portal
entry.content: What setting is required to make the Production version of a document show by default.  At the moment, I get either Draft or Test, and that is quite annoying.

entry.title: Re: HTTP Content Server all communication via SAP server?
entry.content:
Hello, 

   If you want all the requests to the content server to go via SAP Application Server,  You make sure that RFC destination 'SAPHTTP' is not accessible. You can also try user parameter 'SAPHTTP_RFC_CHECK_TO' VALUE 'FAILED' This will make sure that SAPHTTP destination will not be called for a that user.

Regards

SSB

entry.title: Re: G/L account renumbering for secondary cost elements
entry.content:
Dear Jeremy Deo,

Thank you very much for your detail clarification.

It is an issue for my customer not permitted for Renunbering secondary cost elements.
The legacy system accounts includes the account number which starts 90xxxxxx, 92xxxx, 9509xx with 6 digit.

If the renumbering is not permitted, does it mean that customer have to define All the G/L account number length as 8 digit on SAP system ?

I would appreciate for your support, 
Ai

entry.title: [Free Tier] No IAS tenant available when trying to establish trust (SAP BTP)
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm using SAP BTP Free Tier (Pay-as-you-go) and trying to subscribe to services like SAP Build Work Zone and Launchpad.

However, when I try to "Establish Trust" in the subaccount, **no identity Authentication tenant is listed** in the tenant selection.

The screen just shows "No available tenant"

I alreay created an instance of "Cloud Identity Services(default)" in my subaccount. I also received an S-user via email.

Still, I cannot proceed because no IAS tenant is available for selection.

Here ar my details.

- Global Account ID: S0027108224

- Subaccount: dorandev

- Region: AWS South Korea

- Email: doran@doranlab.dev

How can I request or link an IAS tenant for this subaccount? Any help would be appreciated!

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Export Control Clarification Request for SAP S/4HANA...
entry.content: Hi Tsvetinka, Thank you for your message. I am currently requesting to have the non-Latin characters in my profile corrected. Once the update is complete, I will try again and let you know the result. Best regards, a5ymnk

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting Preparation Ledger: A Closer Look
entry.content: Thanks.

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori: Tips: Download list of all Fiori Apps in Excel format from Reference Library
entry.content: yes very helpful!

entry.title: Re: Custom MDF Analysis Dimension problem - Neuroinclusion
entry.content:
Hi @DariusSoreata - Lookups tab is provided in you need to map table without User ID or Person ID available to other table that has User ID/Person ID available.  There are examples already in customer configuration that you can check.  If External Code is not User ID or Person ID, do not just setup this field as Primary Person or Secondary Person.   Primary Person or Secondary Person special fields need to be consistent, i.e. if User ID is setup as Primary Person then other Primary Person should only contain User ID value, otherwise it will cause issue during processing.

You need to understand the MDF tables and how they link to user related records in Job Info.  This I cannot help as you need to find out yourself.  Then use Lookups tab to link these tables together.

entry.title: Not able to delete or remove an Exclusion Rules from Configure Price Strategy
entry.content:
Hello SAP Team,

could you please advise if we can delete exclusion rules, that are not required anymore?

I can only set them to blocked.

Reproduction:
- Enter Business configuration; search for fine-tuning activity "configure price strategy"
- select "Exclusion Rules"
-> There is no function to delete blocked exclusion rules


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Not able to delete or remove an Exclusion Rules from...
entry.content:
Dear Requester,

Once the rule is activated into Active status, it is not possible to delete or remove it. The button of Remove is grayed out.

Removal of a rule is possible when the rule is In Preparation status.

If the exclusion rule is activated and then not needed, set it to Blocked status by clicking Action button then choose Block Rule option.

Note, once the rule is Blocked status, no more action could be done to it, which means the rule can not be reverted back to Active status.

 

Thanks,

Adie

entry.title: Re: Time confirmation with two assign 2 activity types to...
entry.content: Hi Ndubuisi_PALMER, Thank you for your response. We share the same perspective, and it has been confirmed. However, it seems that SAP did not fully consider scenarios where unplanned activity types or work centers might need to confirm the same order operation. In IW41, you can post confirmations for a specific activity type as long as the associated work center is entered. For example, activity types A and B can each have their own work centers, and the system allows this. However, in the FIORI app Find Maintenance Order and Operations with the mass upload time confirmation feature, the upload template only includes the activity type column—there is no field for the work center. This limitation prevents us from performing the type of confirmations that are possible in the SAP GUI.

entry.title: Re: Reconciliation Balances on the fly translation to euro
entry.content:
Hi @Gabor5 

These are "converted measures", which are virtual measures you create in Define Reconciliation Cases app. See SAP help:  Define Reconciliation Cases.

@Chuma , scope item is 40Y, not 2CX.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Thanks! Grace

entry.title: Re: Change GL and Movement Type manually when request mat...
entry.content:
Dear Jeremy,

Thank you for your suggestions. I would like to know if I cannot change MVT and GL,

Normally, when issuing material to a PM order, the GL account is derived from the OBYC settings, which provide a single predefined account. However, I would like the system to post to a different GL account that I specify by setting up a substitution rule. Is it possible to achieve this using substitution in the S/4HANA Public Cloud version?

Best egards,

Keratiya D.

entry.title: El role SAP_BR_MAINTENACE_PLANNER no muestra los enlaces ni los aplicativos WDA
entry.content:
Hi I'm preparing some EAM workshops and suddenly the system can't show me the links and when I try to add them the page crashes:

Los enlaces estándar no aparecen

al dar clic se bloquea la pagina

The role has been reviewed and reloaded, but this situation remains the same, In addition the WDA applications to create and modify master data, notifications and orders have disappeared and I cannot attach then via catalog.

entry.title: Limit Item not show time period in PO
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

I am currently working with limit items in S/4HANA Public Cloud, which have replaced framework orders from ECC 6.0. In ECC, we were able to maintain a validity period (start and end dates) at the PO header level when converting a framework-type purchase requisition to a PO. This functionality allowed us to monitor timelines and extract the validity period into the PO output form.

Howevr, in the Public Cloud version, while the limit amount is shown at the item level, the validity dates are no longer displayed at the header level.

Since I need to define a timeline to monitor framework-type POs and also reflect this information in the PO form output, could you please help verify where this data can be maintained or if there is an alternative approach in the Public Cloud?

Thank you for your support.

 

Best regards,
Keratiya D.

entry.title: Cross-plant CM is not same with plant-specific configuration
entry.content:
Hello,

As you can see, for the material PS65811050000, we have cross-plant CM from basic data 2 and plant-specific configuration from MRP 3.

However, both of them from configurable material PS6581, but the configuration structure is different.

I check class for PS6581, the structure in plant-specific configuration is correct. The structure in cross-plant CM is from another class.

Could you please explain about why it can happen?

entry.title: Re: Why I cannot find the project template when I create...
entry.content:
Dear Ken_Melching,

Thanks for your comments.

My main problem is that I couldn't find the 'Project Template ID' in the configuration.

But I have created Project Template ID in PPM

So, please kindly tell me the right way to find this Project Template ID.

Regards

Vincent

entry.title: Inaktive mass maintennce plan in sap
entry.content:
hi,

there is others way to inactive massmaintennce plan in sap?

thanks

entry.title: Re: Streamlining Process with SAP's Mass Maintenance Order Confirmation: A Guide to Efficient Order
entry.content:
Hi RAHEEL_BABAR

Thank you for this information.

I have one question, why would be the template is containing column for Activity Type rather that the Work Center. For unplanned confirmation to other activity type means another work center as well, you cannot do it. Seems to be a limitation. While you can still do it in GUI app IW41, confirming an unplanned work by a different work center/activity type. You can only do it if you enter the work center and the activity type will be fill-in automatically based on the master data mapping of both.

Hopefully, there could be improvement on that aspect.

entry.title: EDI in S4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi All

IDOCs were used for EDI mapping and communication with Business Partners in S4HANA Private Cloud and older versions of SAP ERP. 

What is the recommended way to map EDIs for S4HANA Public Cloud? Are IDOCs available or can we use APIs for the mapping?

Regards

Lakshmi

entry.title: Re: HANA Memory KPI used in CALM not same as SAP4Me
entry.content:
Hi Chuma,

Thank you for the response! 

Regards

Lakshmi

entry.title: Re: How Loading date is calculated in sale order
entry.content: I have already answered to your query and I presume, you did not go through the explanation under "Procedure".  System will consider those hours.  Having said that, you need to check first Shipping Point what is set against Loading Time, Pick/Pack time etc., then, in Route determination and finally Loading Group

entry.title: Re: SAP Commerce Cloud, Composable Storefront Support wit...
entry.content:
You can also refer to this Help Portal link for update release information 🙂
Changes to Release Numbering and Update Policies for Composable Storefront Starting in February 2024 | SAP Help Portal

Is there a Enterprise Architect Academy like there is one for LeanIX ? If yes can someone provide me the link and guide how to join the same ?

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK Font-Style for iOS - change Font size?

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK Title/Sections
entry.content: Thanks bill for confirming

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK Object table Single Select mode instead of mu...
entry.content: Ok, thanks. I hope this can be added in the future as standard feature since it can add new possibilities to the way we handle table objects.

entry.title: Re: MIR4 BDC not working in Production system
entry.content: Hi Abhishek, Can you please share the BDC code which you have used to achieve this requirement.

entry.title: Re: Auto printing issue while adding marketting documents
entry.content: I have checked all the methods you mentioned and it is not working yet.I am using sap b1 v10 desktop app.if you could explain solution with screenshot ,i would be grateful

entry.title: MDK How can I dynamically select Object Table item on Split control(left) and show details on right.
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I’m working with a split-page layout where the left side displays an Object Table and the right side shows the details of the selected item.

Currently, I have a "Create" button on the left side that opens a modal page to input details for a new item. Upon saving, the new item is successfully created and appears in the Object Table.

What I would like to achieve is the following:
After the modal closes and returns to the split page, I want the newly created item to be automatically selected in the Object Table, and its details displayed on the right side.

I understand that setActionBinding and navigation can update the detail section, but this doesn’t visually change the selection state of the item in the Object Table.

Is there a recommended approach to programmatically select the newly added item in the Object Table and trigger the detail display accordingly?

Appreciate your help!

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: Actual Activity Rate Calculation without using Materi...
entry.content:
Hi  @Chuma ,

please note that according to our Rules of Engagement: "If you want to publish content that was helped/created by GenAI, you must add the user tag  GenAI Assisted Content. For content where it's not possible to add a user tag (e.g., answers), you must include this statement: "GenAI was used to help generate this content." We have edited your answer and added the GenAI-statement as we detected AI-generated content in your post. Thank you for your contribution in the SAP Community!

Best regards, 

Kavya

entry.title: How to filter and analyze based on Ship-To Party’s City/District in the List Sales Orders report?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

We need to capture the City/District of the Ship-To Party for each line item in the Sales Order, as per the selected address in the Item Partner Tab. For reporting purposes, this information is crucial for us. Once this custom field is populated, we intend to use it in the "List Sales Orders" report, enabling filtering and analysis based on the Ship-To Party’s City/District.

We've been suggested with I_SalesOrderPartner, I_SalesOrderItemPartner, and I_Address_2. However, they appear to be not feasible in our case.

Any other suggestions?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Copying Custom Fields from Sales Orders to Purchase O...
entry.content:
Hi @Ndubuisi_PALMER  ,

please note that according to our Rules of Engagement: "If you want to publish content that was helped/created by GenAI, you must add the user tag  GenAI Assisted Content. For content where it's not possible to add a user tag (e.g., answers), you must include this statement: "GenAI was used to help generate this content." We have edited your answer and added the GenAI-statement as we detected AI-generated content in your post. Thank you for your contribution in the SAP Community!

Best regards, 

Kavya

entry.title: Re: manage RFQ app error. Follow-on doc. is inconsistent....

entry.title: Re: Report on percentage billing on each project in a month
entry.content: Thank you for the solution, but the question is specifically for SAP story reports, where some of the operators you have used are not available

entry.title: Re: Fiori Elements / CAP application together with SAP C4C ODATA API - Draft Handling Required?
entry.content: Still facing this issue in 2025. Anyone with answers with what you did? @LuizSouza @Shanir , @thomas_jung ?

entry.title: Re: How to ignore specific transport from Downgrade Protection detection
entry.content: It is good not to re-import the failed(RC8) transport repetitively. Adjusting the Repeat_on_error parameter and correcting the import failures with new transports is a better option.

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori elements extension: upload Excel files to create new entries using building blocks
entry.content:
Hi,
looks like the field that should actually show the file uploader is rendered in display mode. Did you follow the same steps as in the referenced blog and mine? Is the excel property edit enabled? Which UI5 version did you use?
You can test it by adding any other property of your entity in the dialog, and see if it's shown readonly, or in edit mode. You might add another string or integer property into the singleton type, and try to render this one in the dialog. Does this work?

Best regards,
Marcel

entry.title: Re: Adding Custom Tooltips or Popups for Comments/Links i...
entry.content: I feel that the current default behavior of data point comment is quite close to what you are asking for. Data point comment will be displayed as popup when user click the green corner cell of a data cell. Why it's not sufficient for your use case?

entry.title: Re: EDI in S4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hello,

please have a look at the following SAP Blog regarding the strategy for EDI processing:
https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-s-4hana-cloud-edi-integration-strategy/ba-p/13334311

Regards Johannes
Johannes Schulte | Senior SAP Expert
Email: johannes.schulte@vico-finconsulting.vicoland.com
On behalf of: Vicoland GmbH
Website: www.vicoland.com/sap

entry.title: Re: Inventory Opening Balance DTW to be imported as DRAFT
entry.content:
Hi,

normally drafts will be stored in the ODRF.  You can search for it in the DTW-Templates. I'm not sure that this Object is supported there.

regards Lothar

entry.title: 销售合同价格协议
entry.content:
业务需求

公司需求在销售合同中对特定产品品类增加10%的折扣，这样根据合同创建的销售订单都可以获得10%的折扣，尽管单独在销售订单中增加折扣是支持的，但并不能满足通过一个合同收集与客户的完整协议，并且增加了使用灵活性。

前提配置

1. 配置：定义价格维护场景

案例使用了标准配置，客户可以新建条目使用字段物料或者物料组，注意同时需要维护相应的字段表。2.配置项目类别

系统实现

1. 管理销售合同-版本2点击行项目详细，维护价格协议

条件类型DPA1, -5%折扣

2. 参考销售合同创建销售订单

定价协议折扣从销售合同自动添加到销售订单

注意：销售订单的定价程序中需要有对应定价类型，比如DPA1

感谢观看

entry.title: Re: BAPI_SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT2 with BOM
entry.content: Hello, could you please share the ABAP code? I'm having trouble with the BAPI to create the "BOM" explosion of the materials.

entry.title: Re: EDI in S4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
@lvbarat wrote:

Hi All

IDOCs were used for EDI mapping and communication with Business Partners in S4HANA Private Cloud and older versions of SAP ERP. 

What is the recommended way to map EDIs for S4HANA Public Cloud? Are IDOCs available or can we use APIs for the mapping?

Regards

Lakshmi

Hello @lvbarat,
In S/4HANA Public Cloud, IDocs are largely replaced by SOAP APIs and prepackaged integration flows via SAP Cloud Integration. While a few IDocs remain for master data, transactional EDI mapping is now recommended through APIs and iFlows from the SAP API Business Hub.

Best Regards,
Amy Cross

entry.title: Re: Debugging a Python SAP BTP Service Locally in VSCode
entry.content: Can we do the same using Business Application Studio instead of VSCode ?

entry.title: Re: Note-Oriented Discovery of Maintenance Planner for SAP Security Notes
entry.content:
Guten Tag, Herr Görlich!
Nun kann ich selbst die Lösung darstellen für mein o. a. Problem mit "Entdecken Sie SAP-Hinweise":
Es fehlte bei meinem Kunden "Erweitertes Onboarding für Maintenance Planner" gem. SAP HW 2921927" - das hat sich aus einem SAP Case ergeben, den ich dazu erstellt hatte.
Auch hat SAP mit SAP HW „3615614 - "Note-Oriented Discovery" button not visible in Maintenance Planner home screen
SAP Knowledge Base Article, Version: 1, Released On: 12.06.2025“
eine Beschreibung des Problems nachgeliefert - 
dort sieht man auch in Einzelheiten den NOT-O.K.-Zustand sowie den O.K.-Zustand im Maintenance Planner.

Liebe Grüße,
Peter KOSAR,

entry.title: Re: 有没有一个报表或者CDS，可以查看所有物料的历史标准成本和实际成本
entry.content:
Machine translation:
Is there a report or CDS to view historical standard and actual costs for all materials?

entry.title: Re: Unplanned Components Required in Production order in...
entry.content:
1. For the planned goods issue of components, the reservation will exist in the production order (Whatever it's come from BOM or adding by manually), and you should  Enter "Order" in the header, and it will link the reservation automatically.

2. For the unplanned goods issue of components, you should choose "Other" in header, and then enter the material, Qty, production order, and it will not link the reservation.

For the planned and unplanned goods issue, they are different operations in SAP system, so if you don't want the unplanned goods issue, pls don't use it, thank you.

entry.title: Re: EBS configuration problem
entry.content: I have assigned the interpretation algorithm 000 to the external transaction code. When it was 001, the system was not reading anything, and the strings were not working

entry.title: Re: lead qualification how to auto set based on date
entry.content: Thank you

entry.title: Re: For ECC clients what will happen to the Graphical Pla...
entry.content:
Hello @ann_hustis 

Q: What risks will they face in doing so? 

A: Lack of SAP support. ECC is supported according to the maintenance schedule. Your customer should check their maintenance contract about the deadlines. Other than that I don't see any major risks. CM21/CM25 is so mature, that they can use it even without SAP support.

 

Q: Does the S/4HANA's Compatibility Scope deadline of 31 December 2025 impact ECC systems too? 

A: Compatibility Scope or Pack is S/4HANA concept only. They don't apply to ECC systems. 

 

Q: If they discover a bug in CM21/CM25 will SAP support them during 2026 and 2027?  

A: See my first response. CM21/CM25 is and will be supported by SAP according to ECC maintenance schedule. Compatibility Scope deadlines don't apply to ECC systems.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Step 8 Summary, "Comments" in Contract terms document...
entry.content: Ok, so do I understand it correct that it is internal comments and will not be visible downstream in AGB for the requestors, nor for the supplier?

entry.title: how to configure more dropdown options for Manager and fewer for Engineer in SAP Cloud for Customer
entry.content:
how to configure more dropdown options for Manager and fewer for Engineer in  (SAP C4C).

"If the user's role is 'Manager Role', then show dropdown values A, B, and C; otherwise, if the user's role is 'Engineer Role', then show dropdown values D and E"

entry.title: Re: MD04 Report for Mutiple Material in S4 HANA Public Cl...
entry.content:
Hello @Nihit 

ECC and S/4HANA on premise / private cloud feature the RMMD07NEW report. It is like MD07 but with more flexible selection criteria.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: 報價引用其他物料的標準成本估算結果
entry.content:
HI SAP

想請問PE是否支援在報價或是銷售訂單中透過虛擬料號引用其他物料標準估算的結果，並在報價或是銷售訂單上調整估算的成本結構?

entry.title: Re: EBS configuration problem
entry.content: for the example you shared you don't need the search string, because the invoice number is already in the note to payee. If you then use algorithm 001, the system will recognise the number in the bank statement and add it to the clearing information in table FEBCL. You can also use the search strings in combination with algorithm 001, please check which algorithm you have entered in the search string usage customizing.

entry.title: Streamlining Lookup Table generation with Excel VBA for Incentive Management Compensation Plan
entry.content:
Managing high volumes of lookup tables can be a daunting task, especially when it comes to importing data into systems like SAP Successfactors Incentive Management. However, leveraging Excel VBA to generate XML files can significantly simplify this process.

In this blog, we'll explore how this approach benefits users by minimizing manual work and ensuring seamless data import.

The Challenge of High-Volume Lookup Tables

Lookup tables are essential for mapping data, but handling them in large volumes can be cumbersome. Manually updating and importing these tables into Incentive Management can lead to errors, inefficiencies, and a considerable amount of time spent on repetitive tasks.

The Power of Excel VBA

Excel VBA (Visual Basic for Applications) is a powerful tool that allows users to automate tasks within Excel. By writing VBA scripts, you can automate the generation of XML files from your lookup tables, making the import process into Pipeline job much more efficient.

Benefits of Using Excel VBA for XML Generation

  1. Automation of Repetitive Tasks: VBA scripts can automate the conversion of lookup table data into XML format, eliminating the need for manual data entry and reducing the risk of errors.

  2. Consistency and Accuracy: Automated scripts ensure that the data is consistently formatted and accurate, which is crucial for successful imports into SAP Commissions.

  3. Time Efficiency: Automating the XML generation process saves a significant amount of time, allowing users to focus on more strategic tasks rather than getting bogged down by manual data handling.

  4. Scalability: Excel VBA can handle large volumes of data efficiently, making it easier to manage and import extensive lookup tables without performance issues.

  5. User-Friendly: Excel is a familiar tool for many users, and VBA scripts can be customized to fit specific needs, making the process accessible even to those with limited programming experience.

How to Implement Excel VBA for XML Generation

  1. Prepare Your Lookup Table: Ensure your lookup table is well-organized in Excel, with clear headers and consistent data formatting.

  2. Write the VBA Script: Develop a VBA script that reads the data from your lookup table and converts it into the required XML format. This script can be customized to match the specific requirements of your Plan via Pipeline import process.

  3. Run the Script: Execute the VBA script to generate the XML file. The script will automate the data extraction and XML creation, producing a file ready for import.

  4. Import into Pipeline job via Plan Data: Use the generated XML file to import the data into Pipeline workspace. The automated process ensures that the data is correctly formatted and ready for use.

Conclusion

Using Excel VBA to manage high volumes of lookup tables and generate XML files for imports offers numerous benefits. It streamlines the process, reduces manual effort, and ensures data accuracy and consistency. By leveraging the power of automation, users can save time and focus on more strategic tasks, ultimately enhancing productivity and efficiency.

entry.title: ABAP Editor does not indent on new line in SAP GUI for Java
entry.content:
I recently got to use SAP GUI for Java with the newly enhanced ABAP Editor. I am using 8.10 rev5 ARM64 on MacOS. One missing feature seems to be, that when you press enter to add the next line in your code, the cursor is not moved to the same level of indentation as the previous line. It always is positioned at the first column, regardless of previous indentation or control structure / modularization units used.

In SAP GUI for Windows and in the ABAP Development Tools this is different. In ADT the cursor stays on the same level of indentation as the previous line (i. e. adding spaces before the cursor). In SAP GUI for Windows the ABAP Editor actually tries to be even smarter and indent even more if a control structure has been opened. I personally prefer the way ADT does it but would be fine with either in SAP GUI for Java. Currently you have to press the tab key lots of times or use the pretty printer way earlier than usual.

I am not sure if this missing functionality is documented somewhere / on a roadmap. Can it be added?

entry.title: Error while adding a goal - Cannot set properties of undefined (setting 'textContent')
entry.content:
Dear All,

We are currently preparing to migrate to the latest Goal Management. During our testing in the preview instance, we encountered an issue and would appreciate your insights.

Issue Summary:
Employees are only allowed to add goals via Performance Forms (we do not allow them the access to Goal Plan page) .
When they initially click "Add Goal" button in the performance form, they see an error: "Cannot set properties of undefined (setting 'textContent')".
And after closing the error and trying again, the goal can be added successfully without issues.

 

SAP team provided a solution that we need to enable the RBP permission of "Goal Management Access" with reference to the KBA- https://me.sap.com/notes/3204417/E , although the KBA does not mentions that this permission needs to be enabled. 

And we do not think that this can be the root cause, because -
Scenario 1: When the "Goal Management Access" RBP permission is not enabled.

  • A user logs into the system and opens a performance form to add goals.
  • Upon clicking the "Add" button, an error message appears.
  • The user closes the error message and is then able to successfully add goals.
  • From this second attempt onwards, the error no longer appears.
    (Note: This issue occurs randomly and does not affect all users.)


Scenario 2: When the "Goal Management Access" RBP permission is enabled.

  • Since the error from Scenario 1 does not reappear after the first occurrence, therefore the user will not see the error any more even after enabling the permission because it already appeared for the first time in previous scenario.
  • As a result, we are unable to confirm whether enabling this permission resolves the issue or not.


However, this raises a concern that if the error was indeed caused by a missing RBP permission, we would expect it to persist until the correct permissions are granted. However, in our case, the issue appears to resolve itself after the first occurrence, making it difficult to validate the root cause.


Has anyone in the community experienced a similar issue during migration to the latest Goal Management version? We would appreciate any insights or recommendations you may have.

Thank you.!!

entry.title: Measuring AI Access to Knowledge to Eliminate Thousands of Hours of Wasted Time
entry.content:
Organizations and their users often seek AI access to knowledge but rarely understand its full implications.

As subject matter experts or content contributors, how many times do we have to manually click on multiple internal enterprise-wide platforms (or locations) to find the structured and unstructured information we need securely…NOW?  

What is the cost in terms of time and money?

Let’s step back and reflect!

How many in our organization are struggling to find the latest information, the digital assets of record, to do their job as it relates to every line of business?

There must be a better way.

In Deloitte’s Global Human Capital Trends study(1) they found that AI access to knowledge ranks in the top three issues influencing company success, yet only 9% of the organizations feel ready to address it.

Survey responses included:

  • Over 50% of surveyed workers found it difficult to locate required information, and 80% reported needing to recreate documents because they couldn’t locate them in their company’s network.
  • 71% of individuals who found information easy to access, perceived its value as above average, emphasizing the link between accessibility and perceived value.

If organizations are carrying out these tasks manually and at great cost, it’s time to consider a better way  
to bring out the best of AI and search in heterogeneous landscapes.

How Streaming Services are Analogous to AI Access to Knowledge?
We can all relate to watching our favorite TV show or movie.
 
Imagine if your streaming service could only give you basic channels, but did not allow you the freedom of choice and seamless integrations with Netflix, Disney+, Amazon Prime Video, Apple TV+, BBC iPlayer, and/or WOW (Sky Deutschland). This access is expected.

Clearly it is advantageous to connect to multiple apps and systems, pulling information from diverse sources.

❓Question: Would you want to be limited to the basic service only, without the technical capability to get the access to what you want and need?

This example highlights that whether we want access to our favorite streaming services, digital asset, or knowledge, it is important in both our professional and personal lives to pinpoint what we want, when we want it, regardless of where it is located for business or home use.

Beyond the technical capabilities, why move forward unless there is a clear return on investment (ROI).

The ROI Equation: Knowledge = Competitive Advantage
Organizations struggle to define and measure the exact impact of their knowledge access initiatives. 

Consider quantifying the ROI that applies to all lines of business including sales, marketing, communications, HR, onboarding, call centers, finance, operations, supply chain, suppliers, business networks, IT and product support.

Here is a simple equation…

        “X” Hours (to find information) x “Y” Users = “Z” Total Hours Spent Each Year

💡Suggestion: Assess your organization’s activities per line of business and calculate the math.

If there was a way to reduce the time and costs associated with this manual activity, would you be interested?

💡Suggestion: Use conservative numbers to equate the time, cost, and ROI for your organization?

If this ROI formula sounds hard to believe, consider how PepsiCo Saves 5000 Hours per Year with the Capacity Answer Engine.

The Capacity Answer Engine has a proven track record of saving their customers’ thousands of hours, which equates to dollars saved, by providing secure (ISO/IEC 27001 certified), AI access to knowledge in seconds versus hours (or never found manually in the first place).

This solution provides a library of 100+ connectors which require a huge investment to build, maintain and optimize using a federated search and indexing capability combined with Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) (with an emphasis in the “Retrieval”) to bring out the best of AI and search together. 

For example, Microsoft Copilot isn't designed to perform a federated search or index on internal company documents or digital assets directly. However, Capacity Answer Engine, which is part of the SAP Store and Microsoft Azure Marketplace, can provide knowledge to Copilot via Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) to become the cited bibliography for an organization's generative AI.

How AI Can Unlock Knowledge Across the SAP Ecosystem and Beyond
So how does this apply to the SAP ecosystem?     

⭐ When looking at the SAP Business Suite landscape combining AI, data, and applications with the SAP Business Technology Platform, let us consider one or more of the following: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public or Private Edition, SAP Ariba Strategic Sourcing Suite, SAP Business Network, SAP Commerce Cloud, SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central, SAP Integrated Business Planning for Supply Chain, and others.

Each SAP product or solution has a plethora of digital assets found in multiple internal global locations (or sites) that are at the core of running the business.

Most organizations have heterogenous environments, which includes 3rd party applications, tools, platforms such as:

  • Document Storage (e.g., SharePoint, Dropbox, Google Drive, GitHub)
  • Systems (e.g., Jira, Slack)
  • Messaging Platforms (e.g., Zoom, Microsoft Teams, Copilot)  
  • Dashboards & People Search (e.g., Microsoft Power BI)
  • Publishers & Aggregators (e.g., Paid subscriptions, IDC, NielsenIQ)

Limits in the number of data sources and incomplete integration coverage can be problematic. Also, mixing internal with external searches may result in inaccurate, outdated, misinformation from questionable sources.

What is needed is the pre-built connectors from the Capacity Answer Engine library, which are maintained for ongoing performance, scalability, security, and continuously syncs as information is updated, leveraging ID management role-based access control, and ensuring that users have the appropriate access to information and systems based on their job roles and responsibilities.

Looking Ahead: AI Access to Knowledge Will Reshape What We Know—and How We Use It
With the constant changes of market conditions, facts, data, digital assets, products, solutions, services, regulations, compliance, and risks, it is crucial that employees have fast, secure access to the most accurate and up-to-date information.

Organizations that enable its workforce to leverage federated search and index navigation of the constant, overwhelming output of internal organization information (& intellectual properties) will have a higher rate of success with revenues, growth, operational efficiencies, and reducing costs.

Leaders, business and IT experts should consider:

  1. Recognizing the importance of the right information at the right time, anywhere in the organization.
  2. Embracing AI access to knowledge to empower the workforce today.
  3. Prioritizing rapid access to enterprise-wide digital assets as an accelerator, not a barrier.
  4. Optimizing next-generation tools such as the Capacity Answer Engine.

Once this Capacity Answer Engine solution is in place, organizations can consider reinvesting the cost savings (and better use of employees’ time) to shift towards higher priority, higher value activities.     

Many organizations are already seeing these tangible benefits with a growing importance going forward.

Where do you stand?  

*****************
Let’s explore this topic further—what questions, comments or ideas do you have?

  • How would you rate your organization on a scale of 1 to 10 (10 = Best).
  • Can you search multiple videos to find the specific topic or key word you’re looking for?
  • What are your thoughts on the AI access to knowledge in your organizations?
  • Does your organization have federated indexing for AI and Search capabilities?

Very much appreciate your insights! Happy to start a discussion per your posts below.  😊

---

(1) Source: Deloitte Insights:  The new knowledge management - The human factor activates the collective intelligence

Useful Links

  • AI-Powered Game Changer: Revolutionizing Access to Knowledge – LinkedIn | SAP Community
  • Capacity Answer Engine
  • SAP Store – Lucy AI by Capacity Answer Engine
  • Capacity Answer Engine pioneers next-gen enterprise search using Phi models from Microsoft
  • RAG vs ChatGPT  

-----
About the Author

Rich Blumberg is a long time SAP Community member and contributor. For 20+ years he's been an SAP consultant working with SAP ecosystem organizations around the globe. He's the President of World Sales Solutions, LLC, (WSS) a business development and low-code no-code services company. In his spare time, he is a Drexel University Alumni Board of Governors Emeritus volunteer, ENGin English tutor for Ukrainian citizens, and an aspiring guitar player.

entry.title: Re: 有没有一个报表或者CDS，可以查看所有物料的历史标准成本和实际成本
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question.

The Material Cost Estimates app Inventory Accounting - Material Cost Estimates (App ID: CK82) allows you to view a list of material cost estimates in a plant. You can see information such as costing status, costing version, overall cost, and fixed cost. This app provides a detailed view of cost estimates for each material, which can be customized to suit your requirements.

There are CDS views available for analyzing historical standard and actual costs for materials. The "Actual Costing Material Value Chain Item" CDS view provides actual costing run results for materials with price determination control 3 within a selected costing run, year, and period. Additionally, the "Actual/Plan/Target Costs by Ledger for Manufacturing Order - Cube" CDS view. In addition, you can create custom CDS views to extract and analyze historical cost data. For example, you can use the CDS view ‘I_FinancialPlanningEntryItem’ How to Get Manufacturing Orders Check Whether Header Materials Have to look up standard costs stored in the ACDOCP table.

I will also forward your question to the experts.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere Analytical Model Performance Trace & P...
entry.content:
Hi Martin,

I agree SAC also can be a main driver in performance. For my use case I narrowed it down to a simple test story with just one table. However, I think the PlanViz would give some meaningful insights.

Thanks

Christian

entry.title: Re: Recap Series: SAP Community members speaking at SAP Sapphire 2025
entry.content:
Hello @moshenaveh 

Good day, thank you for initiating this. I have shared my learning in the blog. Kindly review.

entry.title: Control and Command keys not swapped in SAP GUI for Java
entry.content:
I am new to SAP GUI for Java and using the latest 8.10 Rev5 ARM64 version on MacOS. To my surprise I am having some trouble with the hotkeys.

On MacOS compared to Windows usually the Control key is replaced by the Command key in most keyboard shortcuts. For example Control+S on Windows to save a file becomes Command+S on MacOS. Surprisingly to me, this is not the case in SAP GUI for Java. It stays Control+S. This makes it very difficult when switching between applications. Especially as a developer who uses the ABAP Workbench in SAP GUI for Java in parallel with the ABAP Development Tools in Eclipse. In Eclipse the hotkeys are "Mac-native". This means Command+S to save, Command+F2 to check, Command+F3 to activate in ADT and Control+S, Control+F2 and Control+F3 in SAP GUI, different hotkeys for the same functionality.

Is there a functionality to rebind these hotkeys or the possibility to provide a toggle in the options? I am currently looking at Karabiner for Mac to rebind keys specifically in an application to get around this but it requires additional approval on company devices and it would be nice if a simple option could be provided in SAP GUI for Java.

Edit: Additionally the Control-based hotkeys are also a problem because Control+F1,F2,F3 are already bound the OS-specific functionality that takes precedence. You either need to unbind those keys at OS level or toggle those using Control+F1. Not ideal.

entry.title: Re: Deletion log
entry.content:
Hi,

SAP Business One uses log tables for previous instances of a document. It uses ADOC for header data and ADO1, ADO2 etc. for underlying tables. Note that you need to differentiate between documents by using ObjType.

BR,

Matija

entry.title: Re: Quarterly Reporting in S4/HANA Group Reporting Public...
entry.content:
Quarterly reporting is part of the standard content of the app "Group Financial Statements".
Check out the available column structures.

entry.title: Re: Requisition Import with Attachments Using Web Services
entry.content: Can  you please give full URN code for importing requisition using web service

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2023 Available Now
entry.content:
Hi @Martin_Böschen ,

Actually, the intention was always every version of S/4HANA Foundation, which is released every *odd*-numbered year.

We felt we should offer 2022, but the strategy was always 2023.

@karl_kessler  explained the strategy in detail here:

Now available: ABAP Platform Trial - SAP Community (for 1909).

HTH.

Best wishes,

Julie.

I will note your point re Orbstack.

Thanks and best wishes,

Julie.

entry.title: “The Unfiltered Truth About AI in SAP SuccessFactors: Insights and Realities
entry.content:
SAP SuccessFactors continues to embed AI capabilities across its key modules- Performance & Goals Management, Succession & Development Planning, and Talent Intelligence Hub, aiming to improve HR efficiency and decision-making. However, alongside these promising functionalities, real-world usage reveals notable challenges and limitations HR leaders must consider.

Note: Based on my expertise, this blog mentions AI functionalities in only a few SAP SuccessFactors modules—PMGM, SPCDP, and TIH.

AI in Performance & Goals Management (PMGM): Efficiency Meets Alignment

The latest AI-driven innovation in PMGM includes generative AI that helps draft personalized, aligned employee goals quickly, reducing administrative workload. Additionally, AI analyzes continuous feedback and performance data to provide managers with insights into employee trends and development needs.

The Reality Check: PMGM's AI Goal Creation

  • Week 1: Users are amazed by the speed

  • Week 2: Managers notice that AI-generated goals sound similar across teams; goals feel “robotic.”

  • Week 3:  Few AI-created goals are technically correct but practically purposeless, and most goals do not align with organizational strategy. As a result, Managers and HR spend time editing AI-generated goals instead of creating them

The Consultant’s Fix

AI-generated goals often require human review to ensure relevance and alignment with team dynamics. Overreliance on AI can lead to generic or misaligned objectives that fail to motivate employees. Use AI as your first draft, not your final answer. Think of it as having a very efficient intern who still needs supervision.

Succession & Development Planning (SPCDP): Data-Driven Yet Human-Centric

AI in SPCDP supports data-driven succession decisions by assessing potential successors' skills, experience, and readiness. It also recommends personalized career development paths and learning opportunities tailored to individual skill gaps.

The Reality Check

AI confidently recommends a high-performing individual contributor for a leadership role, completely missing the fact that this person had zero interest in management. The AI saw the skills match but it missed the human factor.

The Three Questions AI Can’t Answer (Yet)

  1. Does this person actually want the role? AI sees potential, not passion

  2. How will they handle the pressure? Performance under stress isn’t in the algorithm

  3. Do they fit the team culture? Soft skills remain frustratingly soft for AI.

While AI provides objective successor ratings and development suggestions, these outputs depend heavily on data quality and contextual interpretation by HR professionals. AI recommendations can miss cultural fit or individual aspirations if treated as prescriptive rather than advisory. Organizations must balance AI insights with human judgment to avoid rigid or ineffective succession plans.

The Consultant’s Fix

Use AI insights as your starting point for conversations, not your end point for decisions. The suggestions should guide, but not replace, human decisions. Career insights work better for junior roles than senior positions. Career insights help plan learning paths, but they must align with company culture and employee goals to be effective.

Talent Intelligence Hub (TIH): Precision in Skills Management

TIH’s AI now focuses on recent job roles to keep skills data accurate. It recommends learning based on skill levels and improves team visibility for managers. AI-driven learning suggestions help employees focus their development. Workflow updates add control over skill approvals, helping maintain clean data. This holistic view, powered by AI, helps organizations not only fill current roles but also anticipate future workforce trends and succession risks.

The Reality Check

TIH’s AI is like having a very enthusiastic research assistant who finds exactly what you asked for, even when it’s not what you need.

For TIH, the foundational requirement for clean, structured data is a major barrier. Many organizations find their HR data inconsistent or disconnected, limiting AI effectiveness. Without significant data cleanup, AI-driven insights in TIH may be inaccurate or misleading.

  • AI extracts every possible skill from job descriptions, creating overly large skill inventories that are difficult to manage.

  • AI suggests learning content for every skill gap, regardless of priority or relevance.

The Consultant’s Fix

  • Skills extraction works best for technical roles with clear competency requirements.

  • Learning recommendations are most effective for certification-based skills.

  • Ethical concerns around AI bias and transparency require ongoing governance and monitoring to maintain trust.

Strategic Takeaways for HR Leaders

  • Use AI to automate tasks, but always apply human judgment.

  • Keep data clean and governed for reliable AI results.

  • Set clear, realistic goals for AI adoption.

Conclusion

AI in HR isn’t about replacing human judgment; it’s about giving humans better data to make judgment calls. When you approach it that way, the technology becomes genuinely helpful rather than frustratingly inaccurate.

The future of HR technology isn’t about perfect AI—it’s about perfectly integrated AI that knows its place in human-centered processes.

If you want to explore how to balance AI and human insight in your HR processes, feel free to reach out for guidance.

Thank you for taking the time to read.

You are welcome to express your views and ask any queries in the comment section of this blog. I would be pleased to impart my knowledge to you.  Share this blog with your colleagues.

Additional Blogs

If you are planning a  Talent Intelligence Hub implementation, this blog has all the practical insights I've gained from actually deploying TIH in real client environments - the stuff that matters when you're in the middle of an implementation and need straight answers. TIH Implementation: Key Learnings from Client Projects 

If you are a Fresher and looking to specialize in SuccessFactors, check out my blog - Guide to Mastering SuccessFactors Consulting. You will find valuable insights and practical tips to gain expertise and establish yourself as an expert in the industry.

If you are planning an SAP SuccessFactors implementation, check out my blog - Prep Guide for a Flawless SAP SuccessFactors Implementation. This expert guide offers critical insights for a flawless project.
